title: Wangath Temple complex, Naranag
tagline: Wangath Temple complex, Naranag — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

heroImage.alt: Wangath Temple complex, Naranag, Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Wangath Temple complex is a group of Hindu temple monuments in Wangath, near Naranag, in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.
overview.description: Wangath Temple complex is a group of Hindu temple monuments in Wangath, near Naranag, in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. Wangath is a village around 48 miles (77 km) northeast of Srinagar, in the Himalayas to the northeast of the Kashmir Valley. It was built by Lalitaditya Muktapida of the Karkota dynasty in the 8th century CE, and is now in ruins.

topPlaces.name: Naranag
topPlaces.description: Naranag or (Nara Nag) is a tourist village and ancient Hindu pilgrimage site, near Ganderbal town in the Ganderbal district of the Kashmir Valley in Jammu and Kashmir. Well known for its ancient temple ruins complex, it is located around 16km from Kangan, 10km upstream from the Sind River.

topPlaces.name: Nundkol Lake
topPlaces.description: The Nundkol Lake or Nund Kol also known as Nandi Kund and Kalodaka Lake is an oligotrophic alpine lake situated in Ganderbal district of the Kashmir Valley in Jammu and Kashmir, India. This lake is considered sacred by Hindus.

topPlaces.name: Harmukh
topPlaces.description: Mount Harmukh or Haramukh is a mountain in the Himalayas of Jammu and Kashmir in India. Harmukh has a peak elevation of 5,300 metres (17,388 ft), and is located in Ganderbal district, between the Sind River to the south and Kishanganga River to the north, rising above Gangabal Lake in the vicinity of Kashmir Valley.

topPlaces.name: Gangabal Lake
topPlaces.description: Gangabal Lake (lit. 'place of Ganga'), also called Haramukh Ganga, is a high-altitude glacial lake situated at the foot of Mount Harmukh in the Himalayas of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The lake has a maximum length of 2.5 kilometres (1.6 mi) and a maximum width of 1 kilometre (0.62 mi), and is located in the Ganderbal district.

items.note: Naranag or (Nara Nag) is a tourist village and ancient Hindu pilgrimage site, near Ganderbal town in the Ganderbal district of the Kashmir Valley in Jammu and Kashmir. Well known for its ancient temple ruins complex, it is located around 16km from Kangan, 10km upstream from the Sind River.

The Nundkol Lake or Nund Kol also known as Nandi Kund and Kalodaka Lake is an oligotrophic alpine lake situated in Ganderbal district of the Kashmir Valley in Jammu and Kashmir, India. This lake is considered sacred by Hindus.

Mount Harmukh or Haramukh is a mountain in the Himalayas of Jammu and Kashmir in India. Harmukh has a peak elevation of 5,300 metres (17,388 ft), and is located in Ganderbal district, between the Sind River to the south and Kishanganga River to the north, rising above Gangabal Lake in the vicinity of Kashmir Valley.

Gangabal Lake (lit. 'place of Ganga'), also called Haramukh Ganga, is a high-altitude glacial lake situated at the foot of Mount Harmukh in the Himalayas of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The lake has a maximum length of 2.5 kilometres (1.6 mi) and a maximum width of 1 kilometre (0.62 mi), and is located in the Ganderbal district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Wangath Temple complex, Naranag?
faq.a: The best time to visit Wangath Temple complex, Naranag is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Wangath Temple complex, Naranag?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (45 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (152 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Wangath Temple complex, Naranag?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Wangath Temple complex, Naranag at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Wangath Temple complex, Naranag?
faq.a: Stays in Wangath Temple complex, Naranag start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Wangath Temple complex, Naranag?
faq.a: The top attractions are Naranag, Nundkol Lake, Harmukh, Gangabal Lake.
